blurb: First set delivers tight renditions of 'Feel Like A Stranger', 'Peggy-O', and 'Bird Song'. The second set shines with a seamless Samson & Delilah ‚Üí Terrapin Station ‚Üí Truckin' flow into an explosive Smokestack Lightning jam and soulful Stella Blue. Overall show is strong despite a weak Day Job encore.
review: On March‚ÄØ10,‚ÄØ1985 at Berkeley Community Theater the Dead were firing on all cylinders. The first set opened with the crisp groove of 'Feel Like A Stranger' and kept momentum through 'Peggy-O', 'C.C. Rider' and a soaring 'Bird Song'. The second set is the highlight: 'Samson & Delilah' segues into an inspired 'Terrapin Station' that bursts into 'Truckin'', which then rolls straight into a blazing 'Smokestack Lightning' jam, complete with a tight pre‚Äëdrums improvisation that feeds into 'Space', a heartfelt 'Stella Blue', and the rousing sing‚Äëalong of 'Goin‚Äô Down The Road Feeling Bad'. The band‚Äôs energy stays high through the closing 'Around & Around' and a rocking 'Johnny B. Goode', though the encore 'Keep Your Day Job' felt out of step with the rest of the night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the high‚Äëenergy performance, especially the Samson‚ÜíTerrapin‚ÜíTruckin' sequence and the Smokestack Lightning jam; only one mixed note about a weak Day Job encore, so overall sentiment is strongly positive.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear mix (despite bass‚Äëheavy tone) that captures the standout songs and seamless transitions, making it the most complete and enjoyable version.
